quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste animosity persisted over the board, with a series of squalls forming around marriage and genetic preservation.

Anon Questions Marriage to 'Dalitstacy' sparked a downdraft of 44 replies, as one anon posed the question of whether they would marry a person referred to as 'Dalitstacy'.

A heat wave of Aryan supremacy swept across the board, fueled by calls for gene preservation and a new partition. The topic garnered 37 replies, with one poster advocating for the separation of Aryan and Dravidian populations.

Meanwhile, a scattered drizzle of doxing threats developed, targeting Dyaush, the owner of Bharat Chan.

A cold snap of speculative remarks chilled the atmosphere, as an anon claimed that Australia would have developed India if the British had genocided Dravidians, sparking 29 replies.

Nearby, a fog of idle speculation rolled in, with anons guessing names in a brief but dense discussion, also drawing 29 replies.

articles.title: Anon Questions Marriage to 'Dalitstacy'
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a post asking, 'Would you marry this Dalitstacy?' One anon responded with a claim about a woman who allegedly wrote an article complaining that upper-caste men wouldn't date her, and another anon called her a 'woman larping as a faggot.' Another anon shared a personal experience, stating they've had 'soooo many bad experiences' with Dalits, citing an incident where a Dalit woman approached and groped them while working out.

A different anon expressed fear about 'massive hulks' of Dalit women, claiming they 'eye us' and write 'fanfiction about raping us.' Anon discussions around dating and caste also emerged, with one Brahmin guy saying he's only dated Kshatriya guys and finds Dalits 'patronising.' One respondent joked that when women say 'it's just so' or 'I felt so…', it triggers their autism.

The thread also included several criticisms of 'Dalitstacy' and mentions of 'SVBGT,' which appeared to be a term associated with Savarna faggotry.

Some anons also made light of the situation with humor, suggesting that Indra would approve of Kshatriya-Brahmin pairings. The conversation remained fixated on caste and social dynamics, with no clear resolution.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Aryan Gene Preservation, Partition 2.0
articles.body:
Anon started a thread calling to 'Save aryan gene' and suggesting India should partition again to preserve Aryan heritage. The original poster advocated for a hypothetical 'Partition 2.0' to 'save aryans,' referencing Pakistan's partition from India as a model.

One respondent countered that Pakistanis are not averse to genocide, citing their treatment of Shia, Bohra, and Ahmadiya Muslims, as well as Palestinians who had entered Jordan.

Another user sarcastically suggested the original poster's ancestry was tainted by rape, implying they were not authentically Aryan. The conversation turned to the topic of genetics and Aryan identity.

One user claimed putting people in their 'rightful place according to genetics' was the Aryan system. Others debated the origins of Muslims in India, with some claiming they had been raped into conversion, while others asserted that pure Dravidians were Aryans.

The thread also included personal insults and derogatory comments. Some users mocked the idea of preserving Aryan genes, while others argued that Islam was incompatible with Aryan values.

The discussion also touched on the idea of India emulating Pakistan's approach to ethnic and religious divisions. The tone of the thread was marked by hostility and ridicule, with users frequently using derogatory language.

The conversation did not appear to move towards a constructive or respectful exchange of ideas.

articles.title: Anons Discuss Doxing Dyaush, Owner of Bharat Chan
articles.body:
Anon called for the doxing of Dyaush, owner of Bharat Chan, in a recent post. One respondent expressed skepticism about the feasibility of doxing Dyaush, stating that while his DFB account might be accessible, obtaining more information would be challenging.

This anon noted that Dyaush had a childlike naivety about imageboards and didn't know what he was signing up for.

Another anon claimed to recall Dyaush's interest in trains and space, mentioning that he had a Reddit account and had discussed his hobbies and possible location.

One idea put forth was to scrape social media sites for posters who work in space or trains and try to link it with a real-life identity.

Some anons discussed Dyaush's past statements, with one claiming that Dyaush said he stopped using DFB because it was pointless. Others questioned the consistency of his persona, suggesting that he may have been lying about certain details.

The conversation also touched on the idea of using advanced stylometry or autism to identify Dyaush.

One anon suggested checking the archives of Bharat Chan and Defence Forum Bharat to find a user who stopped posting around the same time frame as Dyaush's supposed activity.

An anon mentioned that Dyaush works in IT and might be married, planning his wedding in 2026 or 2027.

articles.title: Anon Claims Australia Would Have Developed India If Britishers Genocided Dravidians
articles.body:
Anon posted that India could have become a developed nation like Australia if the Britishers had genocided Dravidians.

Another anon replied that Australian aboriginals are genetically subhuman with an intelligence cap, whereas Dravidians can have high IQs.

A respondent named Himachali gunda stated that Australian aboriginals remained isolated, couldn't access technology, and thus didn't evolve beyond the stone age. Anon countered by asking what one thing in Australia is inaccessible in India.

Anon also joked that Australia is an old forgotten Hindu land, citing a possible etymology for 'Australia' from 'astralaya'. One anon suggested sterilizing Australian aboriginals and letting the 'race die out', ironically identifying as a Dravidian.

articles.title: Anons speculate on names
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with no initial message. Replies included speculation on names, with one anon noting that "Akhanmal" was named after a Hindu festival, Akshay Tritiya.

Another anon listed various caste and regional identities, including Lohana, Gowala, and Jaat. A respondent joked about "chads on a bike" and another made an obscene comment about someone's mother.

Anons also exchanged names, including "Venkatesh Manjunath Unnikrishnan", "Virendra Rudrapratap Singh", and "Narayan Sadashiv Pradhan".
